behavior_summary: Manages egg objects: when an actor leaves the egg's fast-area, the script updates the egg's internal eggId state; a separate hatch routine can convert an eligible egg into a hostile NPC at the egg's position.

U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea reads Egg::getEggId and, if the value is below a hatched-threshold, adjusts the eggId (inferred as subtracting 0x64/100) and writes it back with Egg::setEggId; if the egg is already at-or-above the hatched marker (0x64) it returns without change.

hatch checks the eggId; certain eggId values are blocked from hatching, one value performs a probabilistic gate (urandom(10) <= 8) to sometimes abort, and on success it marks the egg as hatched (Egg::setEggId to 0x64), creates an NPC (Npc::create), removes/pops the egg item at its coordinates (Item::getX/Y/Z then Item::pop), spawns an AI/behavior for the new NPC, sets the NPC to target the avatar, sets a hostile alignment (0x40), and marks the NPC in combat.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea: early-exit guard if Egg::getEggId >= 0x64 (100) — prevents state changes for already-hatched eggs (high confidence).
hatch: conditional pathing based on eggId values with an additional urandom-based probabilistic abort for one eggId case, which prevents deterministic immediate hatching (medium confidence).

On successful hatch the routine sequences state update -> NPC creation -> egg removal -> spawn AI method -> NPC configuration (target, alignment, in-combat), ensuring the hatched creature acts immediately (high confidence).
open_questions: Why does U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea compute eggId - 0x64 and store it back for values below 0x64? The numeric semantics (staging to a different ID range or wrapping) are inferred and uncertain (low confidence).

Exact semantic meaning of numeric constants: 0x64 (100) used as 'hatched' marker, '1' used for a special-case eggId, and 0x40 alignment flag assumed to indicate hostility — these are inferred from patterns (medium confidence).

Whether U8P_GHOULEGG::hatch is invoked automatically by a timed/event mechanism or only through explicit scripted events (Event 7) in the engine — event linkage is inferred but not fully documented (medium confidence).

main_function.name: U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: Handle the egg item when the avatar (or relevant actor) leaves the egg's fast-area trigger: read the egg's eggId and, unless it already indicates 'hatched', adjust the eggId state (inferred subtraction by 0x64) and write it back.
entry_conditions: Event 10: invoked when the Avatar (or relevant actor) leaves the egg's fast-area trigger region.

function_map.name: U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit trigger handler that inspects and conditionally updates an egg's eggId state to manage off-area behavior.

function_map.name: U8P_GHOULEGG::hatch
function_map.role: Performs the hatch: verifies eligibility, optionally gates by randomness, marks egg as hatched, creates and configures the NPC, and removes the egg item.

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: Read the egg object's internal state/id used to decide hatch/update behavior.

function_map.name: Egg::setEggId
function_map.role: Write/update the egg object's internal state/id (used to mark as hatched or adjust state).

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Provide random numbers used to probabilistically gate hatching and to compute a spawn argument.

function_map.name: Npc::create
function_map.role: Instantiate the NPC that hatches from the egg and return a handle/id for configuration.

function_map.name: Item::orStatus
function_map.role: Set status bits on created NPC/item handles (used immediately after create).

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Query egg X coordinate for pop/placement operations.

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Query egg Y coordinate for pop/placement operations.

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Query egg Z coordinate for pop/placement operations.

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: Remove/pop the egg item at specified coordinates as part of the hatch transition.

function_map.name: spawn / METHOD::15E7
function_map.role: Spawn/schedule an AI or initialization method for the newly created NPC, passing the npc handle and computed args.

function_map.name: Npc::setTarget
function_map.role: Set the NPC's target (likely the player/avatar) so the NPC will engage that target.

function_map.name: Npc::setAlignment
function_map.role: Set the NPC's alignment/faction bytes (0x40 inferred to mark hostile).

function_map.name: Npc::setInCombat
function_map.role: Mark the NPC as in combat so it will act immediately against its target.

internal_calls.purpose: No direct call observed in this extract; hatch is a script helper (Event 7) invoked independently of U8P_GHOULEGG::leaveFastArea (inferred).

external_calls.purpose: Read egg's state to decide whether to change eggId or return early (high confidence).
Write adjusted eggId back to the egg when it is below the hatched threshold (high confidence).
Check eligibility of the egg before attempting to hatch (high confidence).
Provide randomized gating for one eggId case and to generate a spawn argument (high confidence).
Mark the egg as hatched (set to 0x64) to prevent re-hatching (high confidence).
Create the NPC that hatches from the egg and obtain its handle for configuration (high confidence).
Apply status flags to the created NPC/item handle immediately after creation (high confidence).
Retrieve egg X coordinate so the NPC can be placed and the egg popped at the same location (high confidence).
Retrieve egg Y coordinate for placement/pop (high confidence).
Retrieve egg Z coordinate for placement/pop (high confidence).
Remove/pop the egg item at its coordinates during the hatch transition (high confidence).
Schedule/spawn AI or initialization behavior for the new NPC with args including the npc handle and a urandom-derived value (medium confidence).
Assign the avatar/player as the NPC's target so it will engage immediately (high confidence).
Set the NPC's alignment/faction (0x40 inferred to indicate hostile intent) (medium confidence).
Mark the NPC as in combat so it behaves aggressively immediately (high confidence).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Manages egg objects: when an actor leaves the egg's fast-area, the script updates the egg's internal eggId state; a separate hatch routine can convert an eligible egg into a hostile NPC at the egg's position.
